# UC-21.10.10 · Policyholder Lapse Risk Scoring from Engagement Decay

## Description

Portal sessions and policy activities are bucketed into thirty-day windows per policy, visits and key activity types are counted, a short rolling average of visits is compared to the current bucket to measure engagement drop-off, and policies with large decay inside ninety days before renewal are listed in ascending days-to-renewal order.

## Value

Retention and agent teams can reach disengaged customers with proactive outreach, payment assistance, or coverage reviews before non-renewal locks in, improving persistency, lifetime value, and broker satisfaction without waiting for the cancellation notice.

## Implementation

Ensure `renewal_date` is authoritative and timezone-stable; fall back to policy term end if labeled differently. Treat automated payment sweeps as engagement only if that matches marketing policy. **Domain context:** group policies and bill-to employer arrangements show low portal use by design—segment by distribution channel. **Splunk:** Guard `trend_visits=0` to avoid divide-by-zero in `engagement_decay`; filter `streamstats` to policies with at least three buckets when stable trends are required.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Trusted timestamped activity feed and portal session identifiers with linkage to `policy_id`; consent and retention rules respected for outbound marketing use.
• Customer master or policy extract providing `renewal_date` aligned to product and state regulations.
• Opt-out suppression list wired into downstream campaign tooling.

Create search
• Normalize `activity_type` values from multiple systems (`| eval activity_type=lower(trim(activity_type))`).
• Add `eval engagement_decay = if(isnull(trend_visits) OR trend_visits==0,null(),...)` before the `where` to drop unstable rows.
• Save as scheduled report feeding a retention queue; parameterized lead time (`90` days) via macro.

Understanding this SPL
`bin _time span=30d` creates monthly-ish slices. `stats` counts visits and selective activities and keeps the latest renewal target. `streamstats` forms a trailing average of portal visits across three buckets per policy. `engagement_decay` expresses percent decline from that trend baseline; renewal proximity filters imminent term ends. Ascending sort prioritizes urgent renewals.

Validate
• Spot-check decaying policies against CRM notes and inbound call volumes for the same household.
• Confirm `renewal_date` aligns with underwriting system renewal quotes for a sample of states.
• Compare overall portal traffic to web analytics unique users for scale sanity.

Operationalize
• Daily export to retention dialer or email nurture with recommended talking points (billing help, coverage review, bundling).
• Dashboard by channel and product; monitor model drift when site redesigns change navigation depth.
• Troubleshooting: spikes after mobile app releases may invert decay—pause alerts during known releases; fix clock skew on front-end loggers that mis-bin sessions.

## SPL

```spl
index=policy sourcetype IN ("policy:activity","portal:session")
| bin _time span=30d
| stats dc(session_id) as portal_visits, count(eval(activity_type="payment")) as payments, count(eval(activity_type="inquiry")) as inquiries, latest(renewal_date) as next_renewal by policy_id, policyholder_id, _time
| streamstats window=3 avg(portal_visits) as trend_visits by policy_id
| eval engagement_decay=round(((trend_visits-portal_visits)/trend_visits)*100, 1),
       days_to_renewal=round((strptime(next_renewal,"%Y-%m-%d")-now())/86400, 0)
| where engagement_decay > 50 AND days_to_renewal < 90 AND days_to_renewal > 0
| sort days_to_renewal
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (policies at risk by days_to_renewal bucket), Line chart (portal_visits trend per policy_id), Table (policy_id, engagement_decay, days_to_renewal, payments, inquiries), Single value (policies meeting decay criteria).

## Known False Positives

Customers on auto-pay and paperless mandates may rarely log in despite strong retention, appearing disengaged. Seasonal homeowners or travel policies naturally go quiet between trips. Thirty-day buckets can truncate around holidays and look like collapses until the window rolls forward. Data gaps from consent or cookie blocking reduce measured sessions without true behavior change. New policyholders lack three-window history until `streamstats` stabilizes.
